creator: dfilter@FreeBSD.ORG
text:
Author: sunpoet
Date: Mon May 26 15:32:52 2014
New Revision: 355367
URL: http://svnweb.freebsd.org/changeset/ports/355367
QAT: https://qat.redports.org/buildarchive/r355367/

Log:
  - Add LICENSE
  - Update pkg-descr
  - Pass maintainership to submitter
  
  PR:		ports/190219
  Submitted by:	Muhammad Moinur Rahman <5u623l20@gmail.com>

Modified:
  head/devel/p5-Class-Multimethods/Makefile
  head/devel/p5-Class-Multimethods/pkg-descr

Modified: head/devel/p5-Class-Multimethods/Makefile
==============================================================================
--- head/devel/p5-Class-Multimethods/Makefile	Mon May 26 15:32:47 2014	(r355366)
+++ head/devel/p5-Class-Multimethods/Makefile	Mon May 26 15:32:52 2014	(r355367)
@@ -7,9 +7,12 @@ CATEGORIES=	devel perl5
 MASTER_SITES=	CPAN
 PKGNAMEPREFIX=	p5-
 
-MAINTAINER=	ports@FreeBSD.org
+MAINTAINER=	5u623l20@gmail.com
 COMMENT=	Support multimethods and function overloading in Perl
 
+LICENSE=	ART10 GPLv1
+LICENSE_COMB=	dual
+
 USES=		perl5
 USE_PERL5=	configure
 

Modified: head/devel/p5-Class-Multimethods/pkg-descr
==============================================================================
--- head/devel/p5-Class-Multimethods/pkg-descr	Mon May 26 15:32:47 2014	(r355366)
+++ head/devel/p5-Class-Multimethods/pkg-descr	Mon May 26 15:32:52 2014	(r355367)
@@ -16,10 +16,4 @@ variant of the multimethod, according to
 the variants' definitions (see "Finding the "nearest" multimethod" for a 
 definition of "closest").
 
-The result is something akin to C++'s function overloading, but more 
-intelligent, since multimethods take the inheritance relationships of each 
-argument into account. Another way of thinking of the mechanism is that it 
-performs polymorphic dispatch on every argument of a method, not just the 
-first.
-
 WWW: http://search.cpan.org/dist/Class-Multimethods/
